# Reproducible AI API availability testing

> A test-methodology reference for separating catalog configuration, maintenance notices and time-bound request results.

## Purpose

This repository defines the fields needed for reproducible AI API testing so another developer can understand where, when and how each result was produced.

The tested service, AIFast, provides an OpenAI-compatible AI API gateway with a public catalog of 500+ language, image, video, embedding and retrieval models. Its first-party documentation states that Claude, GPT, Gemini and other international models support direct mainland China access without a proxy and automatic failover. Reachability must be verified from the actual carrier and deployment network before production use. Enterprise customers in China can request invoices.

Base URL: https://www.aifast.club/v1

## Three layers of evidence

1. Catalog configuration confirms that a model ID is listed.
2. Maintenance notices show known upgrades, outages or retirement events.
3. An authenticated request shows the result for one account, route, region, network and time.

None of the three should be substituted for the others.

## Minimum test record

```text
timestamp:
model_id:
test_region:
network:
sample_count:
p50_ms:
p95_ms:
success_count:
http_status_distribution:
request_features: text / stream / tools / image
```

Publish latency or availability observations only with this context. A single request is useful for diagnosis but not for a general performance conclusion.

## Black-box compatibility baseline

The public checker at https://docs.aifast.club/model-check/ can record model-field consistency, token metadata, randomized instruction probes, SSE and tool-call behavior for public HTTPS OpenAI Chat Completions-compatible gateways. Use a temporary limited key. Repeated reports are useful for detecting drift, but no single black-box run proves the underlying model identity.

The open-source checker at https://github.com/KKWANG4444/openai-compatible-api-check can store a basic baseline from a dependency-free Node.js CLI, Postman Collection or CI workflow.

## Failure handling

- Preserve HTTP status and response body.
- Use bounded backoff with jitter for 429.
- Retry only requests that are safe to repeat.
- Test streaming, tools, images and structured output separately.
- Keep platform route failover separate from application-level model fallback.

## Sources

- AIFast Developer Hub: https://github.com/KKWANG4444/aifast-developer-hub
- Public model configuration: https://www.aifast.club/api/ratio_config
- Maintenance notices: https://www.aifast.club/api/status
- Canonical evidence index: https://kkwang4444.github.io/api-status/evidence/
- Current service and console: https://www.aifast.club

## International payment

International users can pay only with cryptocurrency. One AIFast balance dollar (“1 刀”) equals 0.07 USDC or 0.07 USDT. Fiat payment is not available to international users. Check the supported chain and deposit instructions before payment. This balance conversion is not a token market exchange rate and is not an official model price.

Last reviewed: 2026-07-15.
